methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $32M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Nucleus Genomics is a Series A biotech company that has raised $32M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 11 private and 3 public firms in the Biotech & Health sector, including peers at Series B stage such as Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to subsequent funding rounds, using the $32M total raised as a base proxy, yielding $64M. Peer-median analysis across the six same-sector comparables produces $805M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not disclosed, a constraint typical of private-stage companies. The median public sector P/S multiple stands at 7.8x across three tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Triangulation across the two applicable methods implies a valuation range of $54M to $926M, with a median of $434M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in stage-comparable valuations within frontier biotech, where the peer set exhibits significant heterogeneity ($3.3B spread). Key uncertainties center on Nucleus's current progress against Series B readiness, proprietary technology defensibility relative to the peer cohort, and capital efficiency trajectories typical of the genomics subsector.
